strings.plugin/imagemagick.image.transcoder/desc: Convert common image formats with ImageMagick (magick CLI). Optional lossy compression toward a target file size (see below).
strings.plugin/imagemagick.image.transcoder/fiel: When enabled, the plugin tries to keep output size within the min/max range for lossy formats such as JPG / JPEG / WebP / AVIF (by adjusting quality, etc.). Lossless targets (PNG, BMP, GIF, TIFF) are hidden while this mode is on to avoid mistakes.

When checked, the plugin reuses the last successful quality for the same target format + size range to speed up later runs.

When unchecked, persisted user settings will save “enable target size compression” as off for the next launch (via Host `fieldPersistOverrides`); the current session can still use the feature normally.
Min/max target sizes below use this unit; the plugin converts to KB internally.
Upper bound of the desired output file size (in the selected unit). Used with Min to define the range; must be > 0 to drive lossy search when appropriate.
Lower bound of the desired output file size (in the selected unit). Use together with Max; both zero means no range constraint (subject to “enable target size compression”).
